title: NVIDIA Q4 Fiscal 2026 Earnings Highlights
text: NVIDIA reported record fourth quarter fiscal 2026 revenue of $68.1 billion for the quarter ended January 25, 2026, up 20% from the prior quarter and up 73% year over year. Data Center revenue was a record $62.3 billion, up 22% sequentially and up 75% from a year earlier, driven by accelerated computing and AI demand. GAAP gross margin was 75.0%, GAAP net income was $43.0 billion, and GAAP diluted EPS was $1.76. Non-GAAP gross margin was 75.2%, non-GAAP net income was $39.6 billion, and non-GAAP diluted EPS was $1.62. For fiscal 2026, total revenue was $215.9 billion, up 65%, and Data Center full-year revenue rose 68% to $193.7 billion. NVIDIA also guided for first quarter fiscal 2027 revenue of $78.0 billion, plus or minus 2%, while assuming no Data Center compute revenue from China. This earnings evidence is useful because it shows extremely strong AI infrastructure demand, high profitability, and continued growth expectations, while also highlighting China export-control risk.
